governing_requirement: Mode B requires a localhost-only health/capability/predict/refine exchange with bounded per-request deadlines, exact canonical request hashing and idempotency, closed validated receipts, deterministic typed errors, and raw predict/refine authority capped at draft until a separate exact-output certification transaction succeeds.

ownership_boundary.MaskFactory: Own the localhost service, contract-native request admission and receipt/error production, provider execution, draft authority ceiling, and producer conformance fixtures.
ownership_boundary.Comfy_UI_Main: Own the external Mode B client, adopted endpoint/capability pins, deadlines, retries, controller integration, and consumer receipt/error validation.
ownership_boundary.prohibited: Thin ComfyUI multipart calls and raw FastAPI detail responses cannot be credited as the production adapter exchange.

prerequisites: MF-P6-09.06 has no accepted executable bridge-error decision layer.
MF-P6-11.01 has no accepted external adapter/conformance boundary.
The live registry has zero configured champion roles, so /predict can prove safe refusal but not champion-backed execution.
verified_foundations: The pinned service is live on exact 127.0.0.1:8765 with health status ok, Mode B API version 1.0.0, a readable serve_mode_b GPU lock, and 17 governed model records.
The API exposes health, models, predict, and refine endpoints and the CLI is constrained to loopback.
Prediction requires the GPU lease and a configured champion provider; the live zero-champion service refuses prediction instead of silently substituting another route.
Provider outputs are serialized under a request lock, checked for exact label set, dimensions, and binary masks, and carry governed serving provenance.
Raw predict and refine results are explicitly draft_model_generated, machine_candidate, and not_certified; a refinement cannot self-promote.

Frozen request, receipt, and 37-code bridge-error schemas plus semantic validators cover authentication, canonical hashes, idempotency, ownership, transforms, resources, authority ceilings, and deterministic error policy.

commands: python -m ruff check src/maskfactory/serve/api.py src/maskfactory/serve/comfy_export.py src/maskfactory/validation.py tests/test_serve_api.py tests/test_mask_bridge_contracts_v1.py
live multipart POST /predict against the zero-champion service
in-memory supported, unsupported-label, malformed-image, raw-receipt-schema, and simulated-timeout probes

observed_body.detail: champion prediction provider is not configured

direct_probes.interpretation: The service fails safely, but the response is not a typed mask_bridge_error and carries no request/correlation/release/capability identity.
A raw successful response is draft-only but is not the signed frozen mask_acquisition_receipt.

direct_probes.observed_exception: ServingError: unknown ontology label requested: not_a_real_label
direct_probes.gap: The runtime detects the condition, but the HTTP/client boundary does not emit a validated LABEL_UNSUPPORTED bridge record.

direct_probes.observed_exception: ServingError: request image is not a readable raster
direct_probes.gap: The runtime detects the condition, but the HTTP/client boundary does not emit a validated INVALID_REQUEST or MALFORMED_RESPONSE bridge record.

direct_probes.observed_exception: ComfyPackageError: MaskFactory API unavailable; run `maskfactory serve --port 8765`: simulated deadline
direct_probes.gap: Timeout is collapsed into a generic node error with no TIMEOUT code, request ID, retry policy, or deadline evidence.

confirmed_gaps: No production Mode B client performs health and capability preflight, version/release/capability/revocation pinning, or refine calls through the external adapter.
The thin predict node uses one hard-coded 120-second urllib timeout; it has no per-request deadline/resource envelope, cancellation, connect/read split, retry budget, or circuit-breaker integration.

The multipart API accepts raw image/labels/inpaint fields rather than the authenticated, canonical, signed mask_acquisition_request and does not enforce request hash, idempotency, replay, owner, transform, protected-region, intended-use, or compatibility fields.
Successful raw responses are not signed mask_acquisition_receipts and omit request/release/capability/provider-bundle/QA/lineage/transform/use-eligibility identities required by the frozen schema.

All service-domain exceptions are mapped to generic HTTP 503 detail strings; no closed mask_bridge_error is emitted for unavailable, timeout, unsupported label, malformed response, resource, authority, or invariant failures.

The client checks a few response fields but does not schema-validate a receipt, recompute hashes, validate a producer signature, enforce authority ceiling independently, or reject extra unknown fields.

No same-key/same-body replay, same-key/different-body conflict, deadline expiry, malformed/extra response, unsupported-label, service restart, or timeout fault drill crosses the actual client/service boundary.
Champion-backed predict cannot be claimed until the three required role champions are promoted and configured.

implementation_packet.objective: Implement the producer-owned contract-native Mode B admission/receipt/error service over the frozen request/receipt/error schemas, preserving localhost and draft-only defaults; Main's client remains a separately owned cross-project dependency.
implementation_packet.path_state: The five additive paths are absent and clean; api.py exists and is clean. The scope is disjoint from frozen v1 schemas, preserved MF-P6-08.05 dirty paths, and prepared MF-P6-11.01/11.02 additive paths. Existing frozen schemas and validation.py are read-only candidates.
validator_commands: python -m pytest -q tests/test_mode_b_service.py tests/test_serve_api.py tests/test_mask_bridge_contracts_v1.py -k 'mode_b or request or receipt or error or timeout'
python -m ruff check src/maskfactory/bridge/mode_b_service.py src/maskfactory/serve/api.py tools/check_mode_b_service.py tests/test_mode_b_service.py
python -m black --check src/maskfactory/bridge/mode_b_service.py src/maskfactory/serve/api.py tools/check_mode_b_service.py tests/test_mode_b_service.py
acceptance_criteria: Admit only trusted, current, canonical signed mask_acquisition_request documents after exact release/capability/API/ontology/provider/revocation compatibility and deadline/resource checks.
Enforce an atomic idempotency ledger: same key plus same canonical body returns the original result; same key plus different body fails without execution.
Resolve the exact source bytes/decoded pixels, owner/person/instance, transform, target/protected inputs, requested labels, and parent authority before provider execution.
Return only a trusted signed closed mask_acquisition_receipt or mask_bridge_error; generic detail strings are never the production exchange.
Map every service failure deterministically to the accepted error decision policy, including retryability, affected scope, remediation, no-fallback reason, and exact request/correlation identity.

Raw predictions and refinements remain draft/machine_candidate/not_certified; only a later separate exact-output certificate may change operational eligibility, and descendants never inherit stronger authority.

Observe request deadline during queue and execution, enforce resource/GPU lease limits, serialize safely, and record exact timings, route/provider artifacts, output bytes, QA, lineage, and hashes in receipt-last evidence.

Fault tests cover unavailable service, connect/read timeout, cancellation, unsupported label, malformed/extra request and response, wrong dimensions/binary values, stale release/capability/revocation, duplicate/collision, crash boundaries, and zero/partial champions.
Producer conformance fixtures publish exact service/OpenAPI/schema hashes for the Main client; MaskFactory does not implement or sign Main controller state.
Frozen v1 contracts, preserved dirty paths, worker records, and unrelated live services remain byte-identical.

claim_boundary: This audit proves localhost service health, safe zero-champion refusal, draft-only authority, provider output guards, and frozen contract/error foundations only. It does not implement the Main client, contract-native service exchange, signed receipt/error production, timeout/idempotency/recovery behavior, champion-backed prediction, or MF-P6-11.03 completion.
